note: Rule summaries are original paraphrases for tooling purposes, not the licensed MISRA text. Consult the official MISRA C:2012 document for normative wording.

Rule 9.1.summary: An automatic object must be assigned a value before it is read.
Rule 9.1.fix: Initialize the object at its declaration, or prove every path assigns it before first use.

Rule 12.5.summary: sizeof must not be applied to a function parameter declared as an array.
Rule 12.5.fix: An array parameter decays to a pointer, so sizeof yields the pointer size, not the array size. Pass the element count as a separate parameter and use that.

Rule 13.6.summary: The operand of sizeof must not contain any side effect.
Rule 13.6.fix: sizeof does not evaluate its operand (outside VLAs), so the side effect silently never happens. Hoist the expression out and take sizeof of the resulting object or its type.

Rule 17.3.summary: A function must not be called without a visible prototype in scope.
Rule 17.3.fix: Include the header that declares the function, or add a prototype before the call. Without one the compiler cannot check argument types and will assume int.

Rule 17.4.summary: Every exit path of a non-void function must return a value explicitly.
Rule 17.4.fix: Add an explicit return with a value on every path, including the fall-through at the end of the function body.

Rule 17.6.summary: An array parameter's brackets must not contain 'static' or a type qualifier.
Rule 17.6.fix: Write the parameter as a plain pointer or unqualified array. The bracketed static/qualifier form asserts a guarantee the compiler cannot check and the caller may violate.

Rule 19.1.summary: An object must not be assigned or copied to an object whose storage overlaps it.
Rule 19.1.fix: Copy through a temporary, or use memmove, which is defined for overlapping regions.

Rule 21.13.summary: An argument to a <ctype.h> function must be representable as unsigned char or be EOF.
Rule 21.13.fix: Cast the argument to unsigned char before the call. Passing a plain (possibly signed) char with a negative value is undefined.

Rule 21.17.summary: A <string.h> function must not read or write beyond the bounds of the objects it is given.
Rule 21.17.fix: Validate the size argument against the real capacity of both source and destination before the call.

Rule 21.18.summary: The size_t argument passed to a <string.h> function must be within the bounds of the object.
Rule 21.18.fix: Derive the length from the object's own declared size rather than from an independent variable that can drift out of step with it.

Rule 21.19.summary: Pointers returned by localeconv, getenv, setlocale or strerror must be treated as pointing to const-qualified data.
Rule 21.19.fix: Assign the result to a pointer-to-const and never write through it; the library owns that storage.

Rule 21.20.summary: A pointer returned by certain environment/time library functions must not be used after a later call invalidates it.
Rule 21.20.fix: Copy the data you need into your own storage immediately, before any further call to that family of functions.

Rule 22.2.summary: A block of memory may only be freed if it was allocated dynamically, and it must be freed exactly once.
Rule 22.2.fix: Free only pointers returned by malloc/calloc/realloc, set the pointer to NULL after freeing, and never free a pointer to automatic or static storage.

Rule 22.4.summary: No attempt may be made to write to a stream opened read-only.
Rule 22.4.fix: Open the stream with a mode that permits writing, or route the write to a different stream.

Rule 22.5.summary: A pointer to a FILE object must not be dereferenced.
Rule 22.5.fix: Treat FILE as opaque and manipulate the stream only through the <stdio.h> functions. Its representation is implementation-defined.

Rule 22.6.summary: A pointer to a FILE object must not be used after the stream is closed.
Rule 22.6.fix: Set the pointer to NULL immediately after fclose so a later use fails loudly instead of reading freed internal state.

Dir 4.12.summary: Dynamic memory allocation shall not be used.
Dir 4.12.fix: Use static allocation, memory pools, or stack buffers sized at compile time.

Rule 7.1.summary: Octal constants shall not be used.
Rule 7.1.fix: Rewrite octal literals as decimal or hexadecimal.

Rule 7.3.summary: A lowercase 'l' shall not be used as a literal suffix.
Rule 7.3.fix: Use uppercase 'L' suffix to avoid confusion with digit 1.

Rule 8.2.summary: Declare and define functions in prototype form, naming every parameter.
Rule 8.2.fix: Give each parameter a type and a name; use (void) for no parameters, never an empty ().

Rule 8.4.summary: External objects and functions need a compatible visible declaration before definition.
Rule 8.4.fix: Declare the symbol in a header included by its defining source file.

Rule 10.1.summary: Operands shall have an appropriate essential type for the operator.
Rule 10.1.fix: Avoid mixing signed/unsigned/boolean/char operands; add explicit conversions with range checks.

Rule 11.3.summary: A pointer to one object type shall not be cast to a pointer to a different object type.
Rule 11.3.fix: Redesign with unions of the correct type, memcpy, or proper typed interfaces.

Rule 14.4.summary: Controlling expressions of if/while shall have essentially Boolean type.

Rule 15.2.summary: A goto must jump forward, never to a label earlier in the function.
Rule 15.2.fix: Restructure the loop or use a proper loop construct; a backward goto creates a control-flow cycle that is invisible to a reader scanning downward.

Rule 15.3.summary: A goto's target label must be declared in the same block as the goto, or in a block enclosing it.
Rule 15.3.fix: Move the label into the same or an enclosing block. Jumping into a nested block skips the initialization of anything declared there.

Rule 15.6.summary: Bodies of if/else/loop statements shall be compound (brace-enclosed) blocks.
Rule 15.6.fix: Add braces around every controlled statement body.

Rule 15.7.summary: An if / else-if chain must be terminated with a final else clause.
Rule 15.7.fix: Add a final else. Even if it only holds a comment or a defensive error path, it proves the unhandled case was considered.

Rule 16.1.summary: All switch statements shall be well-formed (each clause terminated, a final default).
Rule 16.1.fix: End every case with break/return, group labels, and finish with a default clause.

Rule 16.2.summary: A case or default label must appear directly in the switch's own compound block, not nested inside an inner block.
Rule 16.2.fix: Move the label out to the switch body's top level; a label buried in a nested block makes the jump target impossible to see.

Rule 16.3.summary: Every switch clause must be terminated with an unconditional break (or return / goto / continue).
Rule 16.3.fix: Add a break. If a fall-through is genuinely intended, MISRA still requires the clause to end in a jump -- restructure so the shared code is reached deliberately.

Rule 16.4.summary: Every switch statement shall have a default label.
Rule 16.4.fix: Add a default clause that handles or asserts on unexpected values.

Rule 16.5.summary: The default label must be either the first or the last label in the switch.
Rule 16.5.fix: Move default to the top or the bottom of the switch body so a reader can find the fallback without scanning every clause.

Rule 16.6.summary: Every switch statement must have at least two case clauses.
Rule 16.6.fix: A switch with one clause is an if in disguise -- write it as an if, or add the cases that were intended.

Rule 16.7.summary: The controlling expression of a switch must not be essentially boolean.
Rule 16.7.fix: Use if / else for a two-valued condition; a switch on a boolean is a misuse of the construct.

Rule 17.1.summary: Features of <stdarg.h> shall not be used.
Rule 17.1.fix: Replace variadic interfaces with fixed-arity functions or tables.

Rule 17.2.summary: Functions shall not call themselves directly or indirectly (no recursion).
Rule 17.2.fix: Convert recursion to iteration with an explicit bounded stack.

Rule 17.7.summary: The value returned by a non-void function shall be used or explicitly discarded.
Rule 17.7.fix: Check the return value or cast to (void) with justification.

Rule 18.8.summary: Variable-length arrays shall not be used.
Rule 18.8.fix: Use fixed-size arrays with a documented maximum, or a static pool.

Rule 20.2.summary: A header file name must not contain characters whose handling is undefined -- ', ", \ or a comment-opening sequence.
Rule 20.2.fix: Rename the header, or use forward slashes in the path. A backslash in an #include is undefined behaviour, not a path separator.

Rule 20.3.summary: #include must be followed by a well-formed <header> or "header" name, or by a macro that expands to one.
Rule 20.3.fix: Write the header name in angle brackets or double quotes.

Rule 20.4.summary: A macro shall not share its name with a language keyword.

Rule 20.6.summary: Tokens that look like a preprocessing directive must not appear inside the arguments of a function-like macro.
Rule 20.6.fix: Move the #if/#endif outside the macro invocation; the behaviour of a directive inside macro arguments is undefined.

Rule 20.7.summary: Expressions resulting from macro parameter expansion must be enclosed in parentheses.
Rule 20.7.fix: Parenthesize every use of each parameter in the macro body: #define SQ(x) ((x) * (x)), not #define SQ(x) x * x.

Rule 20.8.summary: The controlling expression of #if or #elif must evaluate to 0 or 1.
Rule 20.8.fix: Compare explicitly, e.g. #if (CONFIG_X != 0), so the condition is essentially boolean.

Rule 20.9.summary: Every identifier in a #if or #elif controlling expression must be a macro that is defined at that point.
Rule 20.9.fix: Guard with #ifdef, or #define the macro to a default value in a configuration header before use.

Rule 20.11.summary: A macro parameter immediately following a # operator must not be immediately followed by a ## operator.
Rule 20.11.fix: Split the operation across two macros; the evaluation order of adjacent # and ## is unspecified.

Rule 20.12.summary: A macro parameter used as an operand of # or ## must not also be used with normal expansion elsewhere in the macro.
Rule 20.12.fix: Take two parameters, one for the stringized/pasted use and one for the expanded use.

Rule 20.13.summary: A line whose first token is # must be a valid preprocessing directive.
Rule 20.13.fix: Correct the directive name, or delete the stray # line.

Rule 20.14.summary: All #else and #endif directives must reside in the same file as the #if / #ifdef / #ifndef they close.
Rule 20.14.fix: Balance conditional compilation within one file; a block that opens in one file and closes in another is unreadable and fragile.

Rule 21.3.summary: malloc/calloc/realloc/free from <stdlib.h> shall not be used.
Rule 21.3.fix: Use static allocation or a project-approved pool allocator.

Rule 21.4.fix: Replace non-local jumps with structured error propagation (return codes).

Rule 21.5.fix: Handle asynchronous events through the RTOS/platform mechanism instead of signals.

Rule 21.6.summary: Standard input/output functions (<stdio.h>) shall not be used in production code.
Rule 21.6.fix: Route diagnostics through a project logging layer; guard debug prints behind build flags.

Rule 21.7.fix: Use strtol/strtoul/strtod with full error checking.

Rule 21.8.summary: Termination and environment functions (abort, exit, getenv, system) shall not be used.
Rule 21.8.fix: Report failures through the application's error path; never shell out.

Rule 21.9.summary: bsearch and qsort shall not be used.

Rule 21.10.summary: Date/time facilities of <time.h> shall not be used.
Rule 21.10.fix: Use the platform clock services with defined behavior.

Dir 4.4.summary: Source code should not be left disabled inside comments.
Dir 4.4.fix: Delete commented-out code; rely on version control history instead.

Dir 4.6.summary: Prefer fixed-width typedefs (int32_t, uint8_t, ...) over basic numeric types.

Rule 8.7.summary: Objects and functions referenced in only one translation unit should not have external linkage.

Rule 8.13.summary: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever the pointed-to object is not modified through it.
Rule 8.13.fix: Add const to pointer parameters and targets that are only read, e.g. const uint8_t *src.

Rule 12.1.summary: Make the precedence of operators within an expression explicit with parentheses.
Rule 12.1.fix: Parenthesize mixed-operator sub-expressions (bitwise vs comparison, shift vs arithmetic) so evaluation order is unambiguous.

Rule 13.4.summary: The result of an assignment operator should not be used as a value.
Rule 13.4.fix: Split 'if (x = f())' into an assignment statement followed by a comparison.

Rule 15.1.summary: The goto statement should not be used.
Rule 15.1.fix: Restructure with loops/early returns; if goto is retained for cleanup, it must only jump forward within the function (15.2/15.3).

Rule 15.4.summary: A loop should have at most one construct that terminates it early (break or goto).
Rule 15.4.fix: Consolidate the exit conditions into the loop's controlling expression, or extract the body into a function that returns.

Rule 15.5.summary: A function should have a single point of exit at the end.
Rule 15.5.fix: Consolidate returns via a result variable where it does not harm clarity.

Rule 19.2.summary: The union keyword should not be used.
Rule 19.2.fix: Prefer structs or explicit serialization; if a union is essential, document and deviate.

Rule 20.1.summary: #include directives should appear before any other preprocessor directive or code in a file.
Rule 20.1.fix: Move all #include lines to the top of the file, after the header comment and include guard.

Rule 20.5.fix: Scope macros properly instead of undefining them.

Rule 20.10.summary: The # (stringize) and ## (token paste) preprocessor operators should not be used.
Rule 20.10.fix: Prefer a real function, or pass the string explicitly. Token pasting defeats search, review and debugging.
